hoarder_justification.text: That's {count} now. I have a system.
The pair would be worth something.
Found a slightly better version. Keeping both.
This is the one that will pay off. I can feel it.

death_quip.text: The water bottles were not worth it. Probably.
Recovery certificate: cause listed as 'hubris'. And also a robot.
The Secret Hidden Pocket survived. Small mercies.
Statistically this was inevitable from the moment they packed a fourth bag.
It was a good run. 'Good' is being generous.

hub_gossip.text: Someone in bay 4 is building a shrine out of left boots. Nobody is stopping them.
The vending machine by the hatch queue gave out two MREs for the price of one. It has since been blessed.
Heard another raider extracted with 50 water bottles. They have been asked not to discuss this.
The hub therapist's waiting list is three weeks. Mostly Rat Rating consultations.
Someone wrote 'One More Crate' on the memorial wall. It is now the most-touched inscription.
The locker next to mine smells like expired MRE and determination.

mood_lines.text: feeling deeply suspicious of the quiet
feeling like this zone owes them something

away_summary_lines.text: Several notable events occurred while you were away.
Raider survived without your help. Mixed feelings.
The raid continued without incident, or with incident, depending on the ticks.
You missed some things. Some things were probably fine.
Offline recap attached. Raider status: unknown but probably alive-ish.

extract_success_line.text: Made it out with everything intact. Handler rating: adequate.
Clean extract. Backpack full. Dignity: approximate.
Extraction successful. The loot was mostly water bottles. Worth it.

calm_responses.text: Handler says: 'You miss 100% of the loot you don't grab.' Raider considers this deeply.
Handler radio: 'Believe in yourself.' Raider's pulse rate quietly ticks down.
Handler: 'One more crate.' Raider: 'One more crate.'
Motivational static received. Raider feels steadier and less twitchy.

pressure_responses.text: Handler revokes snack privileges. Raider sulks but considers the exits.
Handler: 'GET OUT NOW.' Raider re-evaluates the risk-reward math.
Pressure received. Raider's survival instincts briefly override greed instincts.
Handler says they're disappointed. Raider begins passive extraction process.
